**Citi on Oil & Gas: Impact Hierarchy**

**Relatively Better Positioned**  
- RIL benefits most from crude price surge amid uncertainties.  
- Upstream exposure provides natural hedge versus downstream peers.  

**Standalone Beneficiary**  
- ONGC gains at standalone level from higher crude realization.  
- Consolidated benefits likely diluted by subsidiary exposures.  

**Highest Downside Risks**  
- OMCs face maximum margin pressure from refining/gr marketing.  
- Retail fuel pricing rigidities amplify downside vulnerability.  

**Margin Pressure Segments**  
- City gas distributors exposed to sustained input cost inflation.  
- GAIL and Petronet LNG face moderate but persistent pressures.  

**Prolonged Conflict Risks**  
- Windfall taxes likely on crude producers if rally sustains.  
- Export curbs/duties possible on refined petroleum products.  
- Gas supply reallocation to manage domestic shortages.  

**Government Interventions**  
- Explicit budgetary support expected for stressed OMCs.  
- Domestic LPG prices hiked ₹60/cylinder recently.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
RIL/ONGC relatively insulated while OMCs/city gas face acute pressures—watch for windfall taxes and OMC bailouts if Iran-US conflict prolongs.

- Straits of Hormuz halts propane and LNG tanker movements.  
- Critical feedstock supplies crippled across Asia.

- Relative feedstock access provides competitive edge.

Hormuz blockade slashes Asia petchem output 10%+ but India's olefins/PVC better insulated—watch for prolonged feedstock crunches.

**BofA India Strategy (Amish Shah): Clear Sector Preferences**

- Strong preference for Defense plays over EPC contractors.

- Telecom, Hospitals, Pharma preferred over Utilities/Renewables.

- Upstream energy, non-ferrous metals over Downstream energy.

- Avoid Airlines/Airports, Hotels, Durables, Real Estate, Beverages.  
- Auto Ancillaries and Tyres also deprioritized.

- Exchanges > AMCs & Capital market plays.  
- Banks > NBFCs, Insurance, Housing Finance.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
BofA's Amish Shah constructs clear thematic hierarchy—Defense/Data centers/Upstream lead while Infra/Discretionary laggards trail significantly.

- US/Israel eye special forces for Iran's 450kg 60% enriched uranium; Trump warns of nuclear risk.

reports.text:
Macquarie on Banks

Farm loan waivers are back

Maharashtra government announces farm loan waiver in the budget

Farm loan waiver of up to Rs 0.2 million for farmers who had outstanding crop loans end-September last year

Farm loan waivers are bad for credit culture, Agri NPLs shoot up

Had seen agri NPLs coming down over the past several years and hence this development is negative in our view

PSU banks to be impacted more than private sector banks

All banks in India have to meet their priority sector targets and agri loan targets, PSU bank customers or farmers are usually more vulnerable

Even today private sector bank agri NPLs are much lower

HDFC Bank agri NPLs were at 3.8% as of FY25 compared to SBI at 8.4%

BofA India Strategy - Amish Shah

Navigating geopolitical volatility

Expect Nifty to deliver 11% EPS growth in FY27 vs street at 16%

A time-bound resolution of the conflict could lead to a sharp relief rally

Rally could extend to capital market plays (AMCs, Brokerages, Exchanges & Custodians) as well as Private Banks and Cement

Recent pullback here stems more from Fll outflows led by macro uncertainty than deterioration in their underlying fundamentals

On the other hand, if the conflict drags on, these sectors will continue to correct further

In case conflict extends for few more days, suggest a portfolio skew across sub-segments within the sector

Would prefer Defense over EPC contractors

Would prefer Coal, Wires & Cables and Data center themes over Railways, Ports/Logistics, Industrials, Infrastructure, EMS, Shipbuilding and Cement

Within Financials, prefer Exchanges over AMCs & Capital market plays and Banks over NBFCs, Insurance and Housing Finance

🚨 The dream of every Indian for the past 30 years, the Kaveri engine project. 

DRDO Chairman Samir V. Kamat said that the Kaveri engine has not delivered the thrust that is required for the LCA Tejas. The engine is working very well; it has given us a thrust of 72 kN, but the LCA needs a thrust of 83-85 kN. 🥲 

However, according to the latest info, the Kaveri engine afterburner test success reportedly displayed increased thrust of 81–83 kN. This happened after the GTRE made significant technological changes. 👌

The air mass flow of Kaveri is higher at 78 kg/s vs. 65 kg/s of the M88-2 engine developed by France. The only drawback of Kaveri is its weight, which was around 300 kg extra compared to other rivals, and now the DRDO has reportedly reduced 100 kg in the latest upgrades. This means the gap is now around 200 kg.

Critics believe that if things go as planned, HAL’s dependence on American firm GE for engines may end soon, making India self-reliant for Tejas LCA engines by the end of 2027. HOPEFULLY 🤞

The government has eased customs rules for export goods that are coming back to India because ships were forced to turn around due to disruptions in shipping routes, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Because of the tensions in the region, some ships carrying Indian export cargo could not reach their destination ports and had to return to India.

To handle this situation, the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) issued a circular introducing a simplified customs procedure. This will make it easier and faster for returning export consignments to be processed when they arrive back at Indian ports.

reports.text:
Solar Industries: Steps Into Robotics & UAV Manufacturing

New Defence Facility  
- Subsidiary Solar Defence and Aerospace Ltd (part of Solar Industries India) has begun construction of a Robotics & UAV manufacturing plant.  

Location  
- The facility will be established at MIHAN SEZ, enhancing the company’s defence infrastructure footprint.  

Technology Focus  
- The plant will produce Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), robotic platforms, and autonomous defence systems, marking Solar’s entry into next-gen defence technologies.  

Strategic Defence Push  
- Aligned with India’s defence indigenization drive, the initiative supports the nation’s move toward self-reliant advanced weapon and surveillance systems.  

Event Presence  
- The Bhoomi Pujan ceremony was attended by Devendra Fadnavis and Nitin Gadkari, underscoring the project’s strategic importance.  

Impact  
- Positive: This facility strengthens Solar Industries’ defence manufacturing ecosystem and positions it as a key player in the fast-growing UAV and autonomous systems segment.  

Key Takeaway:  
Solar Industries is expanding beyond explosives into cutting-edge defence technologies, unlocking a new growth frontier in India’s autonomous warfare domain.

reports.text:
International Women’s Day Insight 📊

Women hold only 25% of mutual fund folios in India and typically start investing 5 years later than men. Smaller initial investments and delayed market entry continue to widen the long-term wealth gap.

The data clearly signals a structural opportunity for the financial ecosystem. With improved financial literacy, goal-based investing, and easier access to investment platforms, women could become one of the largest drivers of mutual fund industry growth in the coming decade.

Empowering women investors is not just a social objective it is a ₹40 lakh crore wealth creation opportunity for India.

Disclaimer: Data referenced from industry research reports and publicly available sources. The content is for educational and informational purposes only and should not be construed as investment advice.

FII DII ACTIVITY 

FII BOUGHT TOP SECTOR IN FEB MONTH

KPI Green Energy: Enters BESS with 445 MW Win

Major Deal  
Signed BESPA with GUVNL for 445 MW / 890 MWh standalone BESS projects across Gujarat sites.  

Execution  
Via 100% subsidiary Sun Drops Energia Ltd (IPP model).  
Tariff-based bidding (Phase-VII) with VGF from PSDF.  

Strategic Milestone  
- KPI's first utility-scale IPP BESS project.  
- First BESS award from govt entity.  
Complements solar/wind/hybrid portfolio for grid stability.  

Market Context  
Standalone BESS stores off-peak power for peak dispatch; critical for RE integration.  

Key Takeaway  
BESS entry diversifies revenue, boosts long-term visibility amid energy storage boom.

Uniqlo's ambition to transform India into a global sourcing hub — targeting 30% local procurement — signals a powerful shift in how international retail giants view India's manufacturing and supply chain capabilities.

With 44% year-on-year growth, disciplined metro expansion, and healthy 15% post-tax margins, this is not just a market-entry play — it's a long-term strategic bet on India's capacity to serve global operations at scale.

As more global brands move from simply selling in India to sourcing from India, it reinforces the Bharat Amritkaal narrative — where India transitions from being a consumption story to becoming a critical node in global supply chains.

🇮🇳 India Issues Advisory as Gulf Situation Evolves; 52,000+ Indians Return Home

👉🏻 Government of India says it is closely monitoring the situation in West Asia and the Gulf region
👉🏻 Indian embassies and consulates have issued advisories and set up 24×7 helplines for assistance
👉🏻 A special control room has been established by the Ministry of External Affairs to respond to queries
👉🏻 Commercial and non-scheduled flights are operating to facilitate the return of stranded Indians
👉🏻 More than 52,000 Indians have returned to India from the Gulf between March 1–7, 2026

Valuation Looks Interesting

Despite strong growth:
• P/E: 13.3x (below 16.2x median)
• EV/EBITDA: 12.5x (below 18.9x median)

Valuations still look undemanding relative to growth potential.

SEBI Disclosure: This content is intended solely for educational purposes and does not constitute a recommendation.

Credit : SmartSyncServ

The Only Concern — Cash Flow

While growth looks strong, one monitorable remains:
• FY25 CFO: -₹91 Cr

The reason? Higher receivables due to rapid project execution and billing cycles.

Receivable days had stretched to ~123 days.

But management expects this to improve to ~90 days or lower as collections normalize.

If receivables improve as guided, cash flow conversion could see a sharp turnaround ahead

High Return Ratios

• ROCE: 33%

• ROE: 25%

• Debt/Equity: 0.05

Strong capital efficiency with minimal leverage.

Profitability is Strong

For an EPC company, margins are impressive:
• EBITDA Margin: ~14.7%
• PAT Margin: ~10.4%

Higher margins come from in-house design, engineering & MEP services.

Execution Capability

The company is currently constructing Reliance’s Campa Cola beverage plant.

👉 Expected to be the world’s 2nd largest facility
👉 Built in just 180 days timeline

Execution speed is their biggest edge.

Strong Order Visibility

Current order book:
📦 ₹1,429 Cr

But the bigger story is the pipeline:
📊 ₹17,000+ Cr bidding pipeline across 700+ clients

Even a 12–15% win rate could significantly expand revenues.

Fast Scaling Business

Revenue growth has been rapid:

• From ₹87 Cr → ₹400+ Cr 
• 105% revenue growth guidance for FY26

Management is targeting ₹1,200–1,500 Cr revenue scale in the coming years.

80+ SME companies presented at Bharat Connect Conference Day 1…
but one fast-growing EPC company completely stood out.

Explosive growth, a massive order pipeline, and management extremely confident about scaling execution.

* 400%+ Q3 revenue growth
* ₹1,429 Cr order book
* ₹17,000 Cr bidding pipeline
* ROCE 33% | ROE 25%

Yet trading at just 13.3x P/E.

Let’s talk about Sathlokhar Synergys E&C Global Ltd.

Key Global & India Macro + Stock Updates (Last 24 Hours)  

Middle East War & Oil Shock  
- US CENTCOM says US has hit 3,000+ targets in “Operation Epic Fury”; Israel reportedly launched its biggest strikes on Iran, including oil depots near Tehran and major missile facilities.  
- Missiles/drones reported toward Bahrain, Qatar, Saudi, UAE; air-raid sirens in Jerusalem; explosions in Tehran and other Iranian cities.  
- Brent spiked ~9% to around 93 as estimates suggest ~2.5 mbpd shut-in, VLCC availability in Gulf collapsed (14 vs 60+ pre-conflict).  
- Strait of Hormuz transit near standstill for a sixth day; cargo movement just starting to resume after Iran’s pledge not to hit neighbours unless attacked from their soil.  
- Trump says Iran has “apologized and surrendered to neighbours,” calls it “loser of the Middle East,” and signals war will last “whatever it takes,” with talk of much harder future strikes.  

India Energy Security & OMCs  
- Govt sources reiterate: petrol/diesel pump prices will not be raised for now, even as global crude spikes.  
- India holds 250M+ barrels of crude/products, giving 7–8 weeks buffer; crude now sourced from ~40 countries, share imported outside Hormuz up from 60% to 70%.  
- Refining capacity ~258 MMTPA (4th largest globally), above domestic demand; 20% ethanol blending replaces ~44M barrels per year.  
- Domestic LPG price has been hiked but further increases are “unlikely”; ₹300/cylinder subsidy continues for 10.33 Cr Ujjwala connections; OMCs absorbing under-recoveries.  

Commodity & Central Bank Flows  
- China’s PBoC has now bought gold for 16 straight months, adding 30,000 oz in February as geopolitical risk and oil shock push bullion higher.  

India Corporate / Stock-Specific  

- NALCO: Promotions of 9 executives to Executive Director level across ops, maintenance, marketing, projects, HR, logistics – strengthens senior bench, but no direct financial impact near term.  

- Sonata Software: US subsidiary files involuntary Chapter 7 bankruptcy against client OBSA (US$10.64M receivable + damages) in Delaware. Management guides Q4 FY26 PAT still accretive vs Q3 and revenue in line, so impact termed neutral.  

- IRB Infra:  
  - Toll revenue across InvIT assets up ~13% YoY in Feb to ₹160.8 Cr; company-level toll revenue highlighted at ₹746 Cr (+22% YoY).  
  - Signals strong traffic and cash flows despite macro volatility—positive for roads/toll theme.  

- RITES: Consultancy + PMC order of ₹45.2 Cr for 4-lane extra-dosed Muriganga bridge (Kakdwip–Sagar Island) with 48-month tenure – adds to long-duration order book.  

- IOC/BPCL/HPCL / MRPL: Govt reiterates no near-term fuel price hikes; MRPL denies media reports of shutting units for lack of crude and says refinery is running normally.  

- GlaxoSmithKline Pharma: GST demand of ₹6.93 Cr (tax + penalty) on export-of-services classification; company to contest; impact neutral given balance sheet size.  

- HDFC Life: GST demand upheld for FY19–20 – ₹104.8 Cr tax + ₹94.3 Cr interest/penalty; company will appeal, sees no material operational impact.  

- Aarti Drugs: Investing ₹10 Cr into 100% subsidiary Pinnacle Life Science (formulations exporter to 30+ countries) via rights issue for capex and growth – positive for pharma exports.  

- Sikko Industries: Plans MoA change to enter energy/power (conventional + renewables), with potential plants, solar, wind etc.; very early-stage but long-term optionality positive.  

- Olectra Greentech: Loses arbitration over ₹10 Cr capital advance to MLR Motors (already converted to equity); must also pay ₹5 lakh towards costs – negative but not balance-sheet threatening.  

- DigiSpice Technologies: Files NCLT scheme to merge DigiSpice, Spice Money, E-Arth Travel, Vikasni Fintech – group simplification and potential synergies; impact neutral to positive, subject to approvals.  

- Vardhman Textiles: Starts commercial production on new processing line adding ~31M meters p.a. capacity at Budhni – margin-accretive operating leverage tailwind.

- Neogen Chemicals: Plans ₹161 Cr preferential issue (10 lakh shares @ ₹1,610) to promoter group entity; 18-month lock-in; promoter skin-in-the-game is a constructive signal.  

- Anlon Technology: ₹0.33 Cr order from Trivandrum International Airport for ARFF equipment; small size but helps niche airport-safety positioning.  

- Dollar Industries: Receives NSE observation letter clearing it to move its composite scheme of arrangement to NCLT—structural step, not earnings-moving yet.  

- RamA Steel: Promoter sells 0.46% stake (marginal negative signal).  
- Godrej Properties: Promoter buys 0.31% stake (marginally positive signal on confidence).  

- Baazar Style Retail: Opens new Express Baazar in Bankura, taking store count to 261 – steady Eastern India retail scale-up.  

- P N Gadgil Jewellers: Opens new Navi Mumbai store (FOCO model), total stores now 69 – supports jewellery organized retail growth theme.  

- Supreme Power Equipment: Bags ₹56.9 Cr transformer orders in Feb–Mar; supported by new Kannur plant (up to 200 MVA, 220 kV) – strong order inflow in power capex cycle.  

- RailTel: Wins ₹26.7 Cr OFC laying order from SECR, completion by Mar 2027 – adds to railway digital infra book.  

- Kernex Microsystems: Forms JV (51% stake) with Bharat Heavy Engineering for advanced signalling (Moving Block + ATS/ATO on Kavach, CTC). Strategic positive in rail safety automation.  

- City Union Bank: Signs MoU with Centific, SASTRA University and nStore to set up AI CoE for banking (fraud, credit, analytics). Good for digital positioning and talent pipeline.  

- Lupin: US FDA inspection of Ankleshwar plant ends with Form 483 (2 observations). Slightly negative headline, but only 2 observations suggests manageable remediation if addressed promptly.  

- Karnataka Bank: Independent director Dr D S Ravindran resigns after shareholders voted against reappointment – governance optics matter, but business impact limited.  

- Avanti Feeds (Q3 FY26):  
  - Q3 gross income ₹1,447 Cr (−13% QoQ, +3% YoY); PBT ₹222 Cr (−2% QoQ, +21% YoY).  
  - 9M FY26 gross income ₹4,761 Cr (+9.7% YoY); PBT ₹698 Cr (+32.7% YoY).  
  - Margin expansion despite near-term topline softness – constructive for medium-term shrimp cycle.  

- ABHA Power & Steel: ₹2.89 Cr order from railway OEMs; total order book ~₹22 Cr – small but building track record in castings.  

- Flomic Global Logistics: Executes large project cargo to Nigeria for 500 TPD DRI plant (850 tonnes, $3M cargo) – boosts credentials in heavy project logistics.  

- Max Estates: Gets RERA approval for “Max One” (revival of Delhi One) at Noida Sec 16B; ~10 acres, 2.5M sq ft mixed-use with ~₹2,000 Cr sales and ~₹120 Cr annual rentals potential – big value-unlock.  

- Elfin Agro India (BSE SME IPO):  
  - Issue size ₹23.8 Cr; price ₹47; subs ~1.03x; listing 12 Mar.  
  - Fertilizer/micronutrient/plant growth inputs play.  

- Srinibas Pradhan Constructions (NSE SME IPO):  
  - Issue size ₹19.3 Cr; price ₹98; subs ~0.09x so far; listing 13 Mar.  

- United Drilling Tools: Bags ONGC order (~₹3.7 Cr) for large OD casing pipes; execution in 5–6 months – reinforces standing in oilfield equipment.  

- Mason Infratech: ₹69.5 Cr order (inclusive taxes) for “Palava Hanging Garden MLCP” core & shell work; 15-month execution, non-related party – strong incremental visibility for a small EPC player.  

- Chiraharit Ltd: Domestic order from Sterling & Wilson Renewable (~₹1.07 Cr) for supply + installation of pipes & fittings in Gujarat – boosts renewables-linked order book.  

- Geojit Financial Services: CRISIL A1 rating on ₹197 Cr short-term bank facilities reaffirmed & withdrawn at company request after facilities were closed; credit profile remains stable.

10.25 lakh crore. That’s what Reliance alone pulls in a year. 

Here’s how India’s biggest revenue generators stack up, from oil giants to banks to IT.

AU Small Finance Bank ; Co Gets RBI Relief on Universal Bank Transition Rule

• 🏦 Regulatory Update: Reserve Bank of India modifies conditions for AU Bank’s transition from Small Finance Bank to Universal Bank.

• 📑 NOFHC Requirement Relaxed: RBI removes the earlier condition requiring promoters to hold shares via a Non-Operative Financial Holding Company (NOFHC).

• 📝 Next Step: Bank will proceed to apply for final Universal Banking license within stipulated timelines.

• ⚖️ Approval Pending: Final license remains subject to RBI’s regulatory compliance review.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Relaxation of the NOFHC structure requirement simplifies the transition process, improving visibility for AU Bank’s move to a Universal Bank model.

reports.text:
PhysicsWallah – Some insights based on concalls Q3

Business Strengths
• Zero CAC model: Free YouTube content drives paid users → very low marketing cost (~8–9%).
• Massive distribution: 250+ YouTube channels, ~3.4M daily active users.
• High engagement: Students spend ~106 minutes daily on the app.
• Strong growth: Revenue growing ~30–35% annually.
• Strong cash: ~₹5,000 Cr treasury after IPO for expansion and acquisitions.

Growth Drivers
• K-12 expansion: Much larger market than test prep; could become biggest segment in 5–7 years.
• Offline expansion: 300+ centers with plans to add more.
• New exam categories: CLAT, NEET PG, IIT JAM, state boards etc.
• Regional language courses: Expanding into southern and vernacular markets.
• AI integration: Proprietary AI models (Aryabhata) for personalized learning.

Key Strategic Advantage
• Community-led growth: YouTube → App → Paid batches funnel is hard for competitors to replicate.

Major Risks
• Dependence on test-prep: JEE/NEET growth slowing in mature regions.
• Execution risk: K-12 schools, AI products, and acquisitions are new businesses.
• High investment phase: Expansion could pressure margins in near term.
• Competition: Global AI/edtech platforms entering education.
• Regulation risk: Coaching and school sectors may face policy changes.

Overall Conclusion
• Market is valuing PW not as a coaching company but as a long-term education platform.
• Future success depends on scaling K-12 + AI while maintaining strong growth in core test-prep.

Jio IPO Delayed Amid Regulatory Uncertainty

The IPO of Reliance Jio Platforms has been delayed due to pending government approvals on listing rule changes.

Reliance is waiting for the government to finalise regulatory amendments proposed by SEBI before filing its draft IPO prospectus.

SEBI earlier approved changes allowing companies with post-issue market capitalisation above ₹5 lakh crore to dilute only 2.5% stake in an IPO, instead of the current 5% requirement.

The rule change could facilitate mega listings like Jio and NSE, but it still awaits final government notification.

Reliance was targeting a Jio listing in early 2026, but the timeline may shift depending on regulatory clarity.

Investment bankers estimate the IPO could value Jio Platforms at around $170 billion.

The listing would be the first IPO of a major Reliance unit in nearly two decades and could become India’s biggest-ever IPO.

Jio has attracted over $10 billion in investments from global players like Meta and Alphabet since 2020.

AI Data Centres Don't Need Wires & Cables Now

Nvidia is moving AI data centers from copper cables wires to optics… here is why it could have repercussions for growth of Indian Wires & Cables players like Polycab, KEI etc.

Context 
Jensen Huang’s Nvidia recently committed $4B to optical ecosystem players like Coherent Corp. and Lumentum Holdings to secure silicon photonics, lasers and optical networking for next-gen AI data centers.

Why? Because copper cables are hitting limits in dense AI GPU clusters:
• Too much heat & power consumption
• Bandwidth constraints
• Physical bulk blocking airflow in racks

So hyperscale AI clusters are increasingly shifting to optical interconnects (fiber + photonics) instead of copper.

Meanwhile in India, Polycab, KEI & the entire cables & wires sector is being pitched as a pure "AI & Data Centre proxy."

KEI Industries CMD Anil Gupta called data centres a "₹25,000 crore opportunity for the cables industry" by 2030-31.

Motilal Oswal's sector note projects Indian C&W companies growing at 17% revenue CAGR over FY26–28. Key drivers cited: real estate, renewables, infra, industrial capex.

Data centres? A "powerful, long-duration driver" — but nowhere close to being the primary revenue engine today.

Even if all that plays out, the absolute cable demand from data centers remains relatively small compared to overall industry demand from housing, power, infrastructure etc.

So the real question:

Is the market overestimating the “AI/Data Center” opportunity for Indian Wires & Cables — especially when AI infrastructure itself is moving toward optics rather than copper

**The NAV Trap: Why Your “Same Day” Mutual Fund Order May Fail**

**The Reality Behind 2 PM Cutoff**
• Many investors assume placing an order before **2:00 PM** guarantees the **same day NAV**
• In reality, clicking **“Pay” at 1:45 PM** does **not guarantee** today’s NAV
• The key factor is **when the money is actually received by the Clearing Corporation**
• If funds reach after the cutoff, investors get **next day NAV**

**What Actually Happens in the System**
• Payments go through **aggregators** like **BillDesk** or **Razorpay**
• These platforms process and forward the transaction to **ICCL (Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd)**
• Even if payment is instant, **reporting delay can happen**
• Example case
• Payment completed at **13:38:13**
• Reported to clearing system at **14:32:31**
• Result: **Next Day NAV Allotment**

**Important Rule Investors Must Understand**
• NAV allotment depends on **fund realization**
• This means the time when **AMC actually receives usable funds**
• If realization happens after **2:00 PM**, order shifts to **next day NAV**
• Execution time and realization time are **two different things**

**How Smart Investors Avoid This Trap**
**Buffer Time Strategy**
• Place orders **before 1:00 PM**
• This gives buffer for **payment gateway delays**
• Helps secure **same day NAV**

**Choose the Right Payment Mode**
• Prefer **UPI or Net Banking**
• Best reliability from major banks
• **HDFC Bank**
• **ICICI Bank**
• **SBI**
• **Axis Bank**
• **Kotak Bank**
• These usually process funds **faster to clearing systems**

**Payment Modes to Avoid for Same-Day NAV**
• **NEFT**
• **RTGS**
• **Mandates / Auto debit**
• These methods may take **T+1 or even T+2** to reflect

**Investor Insight: Market Timing Mistake**
• Many investors wait for **last minute market dips**
• Large number of transactions create **payment congestion**
• Aggregator delays increase during **high volume periods**
• Late orders often lead to **unexpected next day NAV**

**Key Takeaway for Investors**
• Do not rely on the **2 PM cutoff myth**
• The real cutoff is **fund realization time**
• Always place orders **well before 1 PM**
• Early execution ensures **NAV certainty and better investment discipline**

**Small/Midcap Funds in 18-Month Drawdown**

Markets down since Sep 26, 2024 (~17-18 months to Mar 2026)—classic correction for long-term entry.  

**Index Performance**  
Nifty Smallcap 100: Recent levels ~19,000 (52-wk high 19,225; low 14,084); recent 1-mo volatility -3-4% avg, 3-mo -7.5%.  
Nifty Midcap 100: ~57,000 (recent 3-mo swings -8% to +1%).  

**Fund Resilience**  
Top mid/smallcap funds show 1-yr returns 18-27% despite correction; 3-yr 24-25% annualized. Leaders: Mirae Midcap (-3.4% 1-mo, 18% 1-yr); HDFC/Nippon midcaps 19-21% 1-yr.  

**Historical Opportunity**  
Corrections like this (post-2023 peaks) averaged -20-30% drawdowns but delivered 50%+ rebounds over 3 yrs for quality funds.  

**Strategy Tip**  
Ignore short-term winners; focus 3+ yr horizon, quality managers, diversified SIPs. Due diligence essential.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Prolonged drawdowns forge long-term wealth—small/midcap funds shine post-correction for patient investors.

**India’s Mutual Fund AUM Surge**

Large caps >32%; midcaps expanding; smallcaps stable.

Gold AUM +257% (record ETF inflows); silver, overseas, REITs up sharply. Debt shortens for safety.  

**Maturing Behaviour**  
SIP explosion, diversification, less FD reliance—aggressive yet smart allocation.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Retail India shifts to multi-asset growth via MFs, signaling wealth creation maturity.

**Global Oil Chokepoints 🛢️**

​Nearly **92% of global maritime oil** passes through just a few narrow gateways. The **Strait of Malacca** and **Strait of Hormuz** are the heavyweights, together handling over 55% of the world’s sea-traded oil.

​From the Suez Canal to the Cape of Good Hope, these routes are the arteries of the global economy. Any disruption at these "chokepoints" doesn't just stall ships—it risks global energy security and sends prices skyrocketing.

**The Sweet Spot of Iranian Crude Oil**

Iranian Light crude, with its 33-36° API gravity and 1.36-1.5% sulfur content, hits the perfect balance for global refineries. It yields high-value products like gasoline and diesel with minimal processing—unlike heavier Venezuelan Merey (16° API) or lighter US WTI (39-40° API). Despite sanctions, demand persists via Dubai networks, as refineries optimized for it struggle with substitutes. Amid 2026 Strait of Hormuz tensions, prices have spiked to $82 per barrel, highlighting its enduring appeal.

Instead, taxes will be charged based on actual alcohol content, not total volume.

Pricing power could shift to companies.

**Flexi Cap Funds: Active vs Patient Portfolios**

**Churn Spectrum**  
Flexi Cap funds vary sharply in portfolio turnover. High churners like Quant Flexi Cap trade aggressively for alpha. Patient players like Kotak Flexi Cap and PPFAS Flexi Cap hold conviction bets longer.  

**Key Examples**  
- **Quant Flexi Cap**: High turnover style; dynamic shifts across market caps for momentum plays.  
- **Kotak Flexi Cap**: Low turnover (~9%); 59 stocks, large/mid bias. Top holdings: HDFC Bank, BEL.  
- **PPFAS Flexi Cap**: Equity turnover ~14%; focuses long-term compounding with global diversification.  

**Investor Implications**  
High churn boosts short-term returns but racks up costs/taxes. Low turnover favors tax efficiency, lower expenses, steady growth. Category average exceeds 200%.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Match fund style to your horizon—aggressive for tactical edges, patient for buy-and-hold wealth building.

reports.text:
Rising Oil Prices May Push India’s Inflation & Currency Pressure

• 📈 Inflation Impact: A 10% rise in global crude prices may increase India’s WPI inflation by ~0.7–1%, according to a Bank of Baroda report.

• 💰 Import Bill Surge: Higher crude prices could raise India’s oil import bill by ~$18 billion (~0.5% of GDP).

• 💱 Rupee Pressure: The ₹ may weaken to 91–92 per $, with risk of crossing ₹92 if tensions persist.

• 🏦 OMCs Absorbing Costs: Oil Marketing Companies may absorb higher crude costs initially, delaying pass-through to consumers.

• 📊 Macro Impact: Higher crude may widen current account deficit and increase subsidy burden (fuel & fertilisers).

🔴 Impact: Negative
Higher oil prices increase inflation risk, fiscal pressure, and rupee depreciation, which can weigh on markets and the economy.

₹80 Cr capex via debt + internal accruals.

Targets rising auto + industrial forging demand.

**Bluestone Jewellery: Coverage Initiation**  

**Business Model**  
Tech-native omnichannel platform focused on design-driven studded jewellery (2/3 revenue).  
75% in-house manufacturing (Mumbai/Jaipur/Surat) for speed/quality; 300+ stores by FY26.  

**Growth Projections**  
Revenue CAGR 32% FY25-28 to ₹4,090 Cr; 70-75 stores/year (Tier 1-3).  
Store sales/store mature to ₹1 Cr FY30; EBITDA +ve, 10% margin FY30.  

**Omnichannel Edge**  
Digital drives 70-90% purchases ('Try Home', AI personalization); repeat 44-54%.  
Loyalty via 'Gold Mine', old gold exchange; millennials/GenZ target.  

**Competitive Moat**  
Vs CaratLane: In-house prod vs outsourcing; COCO shift from franchise.  
7,400+ designs via 3D printing; data optimizes inventory/location.  

**Financials FY25**  
Revenue ₹1,770 Cr; Gross margin 37.9%; EBITDA 4.2% (₹75 Cr).  

**Risks**  
Gold volatility, expansion delays, competition (Titan/Kalyan), capex intensity.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Bluestone captures studded/lifestyle shift with omnichannel scale and vertical integration—inflection to profitability ahead.

**Nuvama IT Sector: Gen AI Update**  

**Disruption Parallel**  
Gen AI mirrors RIMS/digital waves; Indian IT partners platforms (OpenAI, Microsoft, AWS) rather than displaced.  

**Adoption Phases**  
- Brownfield (80-85% enterprise tech): Phased PoCs in legacy contracts.  
- Greenfield: Aggressive on AI-native stacks; now in RFPs/renewals.  

**Pricing & Productivity**  
- AI work +30-40% billing premium vs legacy.  
- Efficiency gains 15-18% avg (up to 35%); token costs drive opex preference.  

**Workforce Shift**  
Gradual reskilling to AI engineers/specialists; billing includes AI agents.  
Agents shrink teams/RCA time (e.g., cyber: 5-6 eng → smaller with AI).  

**Sector Dynamics**  
Regulated sectors (insurance/healthcare) slower due to compliance.  
TAM expands long-term; Indian IT strengthens despite near-term volatility.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Gen AI boosts IT services pricing/productivity; Indian firms well-positioned as integration partners—near-term demand choppy but structural positive.

RAJNATH SINGH SAYS GARDEN REACH SHIPBUILDERS & ENGINEERS BEEN GIVEN SPECIAL FOCUS SO THAT OUR INDUSTRIAL ECOSYSTEM CAN BECOME STRONG AND FUTURISTIC

For companies above ₹1 lakh crore, the median drawdown was 32% on April 7th 2025. That has since narrowed to below 20%. Similar patterns hold as you move down through the ₹50,000 crore and ₹10,000 crore buckets - the pain peaked and has been receding since 7th April 2025.

But what about below ₹3,000 crore mcap segment?

There has been no recovery. In fact, since April 7th, these stocks have fallen another 3-5%. The median drawdown in the sub-₹3,000 crore universe now sits at around 60% from peak. While the rest of the market has been healing, the segment where we operate has been bleeding further.

Physics Wallah: Hype vs Reality  

IPO Performance  
Launched Nov 2025 at ₹109; opened ₹145 (+33%), peaked ₹162 intraday.  
Now ~₹79: -27% from IPO, -51% from high.  

Q3 FY26 Financials  
- Revenue ₹1,082 Cr (+34% YoY).  
- Profit ₹102 Cr (+33% YoY).  
- EBITDA ₹351 Cr (32% margin).  

Profitability Concerns  
FY25 net loss ₹243 Cr; current net margin ~8%.  
Aggressive expansion (hybrid model, South India, Indic languages) pressures near-term earnings.  

Valuation Context  
Listing valued at ~₹46,000 Cr; now ~₹23,600 Cr mcap.  
Debt/Equity 0.69; FY25 OCF ₹507 Cr.  
EdTech sector skepticism post-BYJU'S/Unacademy issues.  

Key Takeaway  
PW delivers strong revenue growth amid post-IPO correction and sector headwinds—execution on profitability will define long-term success.

**UBS Global Research – India Banks Energy Shock Scenario (5 Mar 2026)**

**Macro Impact – Oil Price Shock**

• UBS analysed impact of rising oil prices due to **Middle East conflict**
• **+10% oil price rise → ~30 bps CPI increase**
• **GDP growth may decline ~15 bps**

• During **Russia–Ukraine war (2022)**
• Oil jumped **$10–30 per barrel for ~6 months**
• Inflation rose **~80–90 bps**
• RBI raised **Repo Rate ~150 bps**

**Scenario 1 – Oil Price Rise < $10 per Barrel**

• **EPS Impact: +1% to −3% (Limited impact)**

• **Loan Growth:** Stable due to **higher SME working capital demand**
• **NIM:** Stable as **rate cut cycle may end**
• **Non-interest income:** Slight decline due to **treasury losses**
• **Credit costs:** **Minimal asset quality impact**

• **Preferred Banks:**
• **Axis Bank**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank**

**Scenario 2 – Oil Price Rise $20–30 per Barrel**

• **EPS Impact: −4% to −8% (Sector pressure)**

• **Loan Growth:** Slight slowdown due to **consumption weakness**
• **NIM:** **0 to −5 bps decline** due to loan mix change
• **Non-interest income:** Decline due to **lower consumption and treasury losses**
• **Credit costs:** **0 to +5 bps increase**

• **Preferred Bank:** **ICICI Bank**

**Banks vs NBFCs**

• UBS prefers **Banks over NBFCs** in oil shock scenario
• Banks have **stronger balance sheets and lower funding risk**

**UBS Preferred Banking Picks**

• **Axis Bank – 1.7x FY27E PBV**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank – 1.6x FY27E PBV**

**Other Buy Rated Banks**

• **ICICI Bank – 2.2x PBV**
• **HDFC Bank – 1.8x PBV**
• **Bank of Baroda – 1.0x PBV**
• **Canara Bank – 1.0x PBV**

**EPS Sensitivity to Macro Risks**

• **Loan Growth −1 ppt → EPS impact −0.1% to −1.7%**
• **NIM −5 bps → EPS impact −1.3% to −3.8%**
• **Non-interest income −2% → EPS impact −0.9% to −3.8%**
• **Credit cost +5 bps → EPS impact −1.1% to −2.8%**

**Most Resilient Banks**

• **ICICI Bank (EPS impact ~−3.9%)**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank (~−4.8%)**
• **Axis Bank (~−5.0%)**

**More Sensitive Banks**

• **IDFC First Bank (~−8.3%)**
• **Bandhan Bank (~−7.9%)**
• **IndusInd Bank (~−7.8%)**

**Key Takeaway for Investors**

• **Mild oil shock → Limited banking sector impact**
• **Sharp oil spike → Consumption slowdown & margin pressure**
• **Large private banks remain structurally stronger**

**Strategy**

• **Axis Bank & Kotak Bank for moderate oil risk**
• **ICICI Bank for high oil shock scenario**
• **Private Banks likely to outperform PSU Banks & NBFCs**

Maruti Suzuki to Expand Capacity to Meet Rising Demand

Maruti Suzuki plans to increase annual production capacity by 500,000 units from next fiscal year to meet strong domestic demand.

The company currently has installed capacity of about 2.6 million units per year across plants in Haryana and Gujarat.

New plants will add ~250,000 units annually each, with capacity additions happening in phases.

The Kharkhoda (Haryana) plant is expected to start production in Q1 of next fiscal, while the fourth line at Hansalpur (Gujarat) may begin in Q2.

Maruti currently has a large order backlog of around 200,000 vehicles.

Dealer inventory is tight at ~12 days, significantly lower than the ideal level of about 30 days.

The company produces 220,000–230,000 vehicles per month, with 170,000–180,000 units for domestic sales and ~50,000 units for exports.

Exports to Africa, the Middle East and other markets are also expected to grow with higher production capacity.

The project is subject to land allocation, infrastructure support, and regulatory approvals from the state government.

Compressors, Pumps & Diesel Engines: The ICE Resurrection  

Did you think diesel and ICE tech were history? Q3 FY26 proves otherwise — India’s heavy engineering sector is witnessing a diesel comeback.  

The ICE Vacuum:  
- As global OEMs chase EVs, they’re exiting traditional engine manufacturing.  
- This exit has created a supply vacuum now filled by Indian firms, turning ICE into a high-margin, defensible niche.  

AI’s Hidden Power Driver:  
- Hyperscale data centers demand 99.999% uptime — far beyond what current batteries can support.  
- Result: Surging orders for high-kVA diesel gensets as backup for AI infrastructure.  

China+1 & Export Momentum:  
- Western buyers are shifting RFQs for Stage-3 and Stage-4 engines to India.  
- U.S. import duty cuts (50% → 18%) are further boosting cost competitiveness of Indian heavy-engine exports.  

The Solar Pump Trap:  
- PM-KUSUM solar pump projects deliver big volumes but weak margins.  
- Fixed-price contracts and volatile input costs (copper, steel) strain working capital and profitability.  

Key Takeaway:  
As EV hype peaks, Indian manufacturers with strong ICE, genset, and heavy-engine portfolios are quietly becoming the new global powerhouses.

GK ENERGY: GST Search Conducted by Maharashtra Authorities  

- Regulatory Action: Maharashtra State GST Department conducted a search at GK Energy Limited’s registered office as part of a compliance review.  
- Inspection Timeline: The process began on 27 February 2026 and concluded on 4 March 2026.  
- Findings Identified:  
  - ITC disallowance on IPO-related expenses – ₹4.75 lakh.  
  - ITC reversal on sundry creditors – ₹1.65 crore.  
  - Blocked credit disallowance – ₹95.9 lakh.  
- Company’s Stand: GK Energy stated that the department’s order is appealable, and it plans to challenge the findings through appropriate legal channels under GST provisions.  
- Financial Impact: Management clarified there is no significant effect on the company’s financial position, liquidity, or ongoing operations.  
- Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative.  

Key Takeaway: GST authorities raised certain ITC-related issues, but GK Energy expects limited financial or operational consequences as it seeks legal recourse.

- MSMEs prefer own websites/social (53%) for lower fees, data control, margins.  

Key Takeaway:  
D2C/MSME-led unbundling accelerates e-commerce to $200B by 2030 — platforms cede ground to direct brands in India's low-penetration, high-potential market.

RBI PROJECT FINANCE NORMS IMPACT  

Key Restrictions (Effective Oct 1, 2025)  
- Debt:Equity ratio fixed at sanction — no top-up loans allowed during project life.  
- Lenders cannot fund cost overruns/delays beyond approved amount.  
- Aims for developer "skin in the game" and financial discipline.  

Real Estate Implications  
- Banks, NBFCs, HFCs face reduced lending flexibility for projects.  
- Higher risks for lenders on under-construction realty exposures.  

Market Adjustments  
- Discretionary funding slows; enhanced transparency/monitoring expected.  
- Workaround: GCP loans with strict RBI end-use compliance.  

Key Takeaway:  
RBI's rigid project finance rules curb mid-project lending, pressuring real estate developers and lender balance sheets — favour completed projects over under-construction plays.

Infosys–Intel Tie-Up to Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption

Infosys and Intel announced a strategic partnership to help enterprises scale AI from pilot projects to full production deployments.

The collaboration will combine Intel’s high-performance computing platforms with Infosys Topaz and AI services.

Solutions will be optimized for Intel Xeon processors, Intel Gaudi AI accelerators, and Intel AI PCs.

The partnership aims to create open standards across the edge-to-cloud AI ecosystem.

Focus areas include model management, data integration, performance monitoring, and built-in security features.

The companies will jointly design, develop, and benchmark enterprise AI workloads tailored to business needs.

AI solutions will support IT operations, developer productivity, and automation-led workflows.

The platform will also enable AI agents that can access enterprise data and coordinate tasks within governance frameworks.

Singtel to Cut Stake as Airtel Simplifies Promoter Structure

Bharti Airtel plans to consolidate promoter holdings under Bharti Telecom (BTL) as part of a restructuring roadmap.

Bharti Telecom currently holds ~41% stake in Airtel, making it the main promoter vehicle.

About 7% stake held by Singtel and ~1% held by the Mittal family outside BTL will be gradually reduced via market transactions over the next 3–4 years.

After restructuring, Bharti Telecom will remain the sole direct promoter entity with 41% stake in Airtel.

Singtel and the Mittal family will pare their residual direct holdings to zero, increasing the public float.

The plan is part of a broader promoter restructuring first outlined in August 2022 to simplify and equalise holdings.

Overall promoter shareholding in Airtel has already declined from 55.9% (June 2022) to about 48.9% currently.

**KOTAK INST EQT ON OIL & GAS – HORMUZ CRISIS**

**Chokepoint Risk**  
- Strait of Hormuz (~20% global oil/LNG) near standstill.  
- India exposure: 50-55% crude/LNG imports via SoH.

**Vulnerability**  
- Strategic reserves: 8-9 days oil, zero gas.  
- Gas rationing imminent if prolonged.

**Refiner Impact**  
- Volumes hold; costs surge (crude/logistics).

**Key Takeaway**  
Acute import reliance signals rationing pain—stockpile monitoring critical.

**CITI ON OIL & GAS – QATAR LNG SHUTDOWN IMPACT**

**Gas Chain Risks**

**Key Takeaway**  
LNG importers vulnerable short-term; pivot to upstream/refining winners like ONGC/RIL.
